title: ulmus minor subsp. canescens
intro: Ulmus minor subsp. canescens, also known as the wych elm or scots elm, is a subspecies of elm tree that is native to Europe and western Asia. The bark and leaves of this tree have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are said to help soothe various health issues. The medicinal properties of Ulmus minor subsp. canescens are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness their medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
